title: Artabotrys oliveri
taxonomy: Artabotrys oliveri belongs to the family Annonaceae, commonly known as the custard apple or soursop family. This family consists of over 2,000 species of flowering plants. Artabotrys oliveri is also classified as a member of the genus Artabotrys, which is a group of tropical vines and shrubs. The genus Artabotrys is part of the tribe Artabotryeae and subfamily Malmeoideae. The classification of Artabotrys oliveri reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Artabotrys oliveri, also known as the Indian laurel, is a flowering plant species in the family Annonaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
